page: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmsv2 server family. Powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8573C processor. The CPU configuration provides 4 dedicated vCPUs across 2 physical cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. A key feature of this hardware profile is its substantial memory capacity, offering 168.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 42.0 GB of memory per core. The instance does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX8-4ms_v2 demonstrates strong single-threaded performance but exhibits limitations in multi-core scaling. The Geekbench single-core score of 2351.0 and single-threaded text processing score of 2826.0 both rank in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also show strong results at 34.8 GB/sec and 14.37 GB/sec, respectively. However, multi-core benchmarks are comparatively weaker, with a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 4441.07 and a multi-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 28.49 GB/sec. Memory bandwidth for large, potentially uncached blocks is also limited, recording 36.1 GB/sec for reads and 25.44 GB/sec for writes, though small cached block reads perform at an average rate of 345.37 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8-4ms_v2 represents a highly specialized resource allocation. The high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides exceptional memory density, making it a qualitatively cost-effective choice for workloads that require massive RAM footprints without demanding high parallel CPU scaling. This profile is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, relational database management systems, in-memory caching layers like Redis, and single-threaded data processing tasks. It is less suitable for highly parallelized multi-threaded compute workloads or large-scale LLM text generation, where its multi-core and uncached memory bandwidth constraints may limit throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmsv2 family, designed for memory-intensive workloads. Running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and a high clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The instance is equipped with 168.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptional 42.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a large 260 MB L3 cache. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core performance, including strong single-threaded compression and text processing, though multi-core scaling and large-block memory bandwidth are relatively weak. This hardware profile is highly effective for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy, single-threaded applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor, 4 dedicated vCPUs, and 168 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-4ms_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 4 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high memory virtual machine with exceptional memory capacity per core and strong single core performance for intensive database workloads.

168.0 GB of RAM providing an exceptional 42.0 GB of memory per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded text processing performance
Premium Storage capable configuration with no local storage or GPUs
Optimized for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-heavy tasks
